Description: Data introductions
Exact 2023 (single stock `h1` and two-stock `h2`) model and data set (model 1.07) from SC11.
As 0.00 but with revised catches through 2023 (currently still estimates)
As 0.01 but with updated 2023 fishery age composition data for N_Chile, SC_Chile, and Offshore_Trawl, and updated 2023 fishery length composition data for FarNorth
As 0.02 but with updated 2023 weight at age data for all fisheries and their associated CPUE indices
As 0.03 but replaced offshore CPUE up to 2023
As 0.04 but with 2024 catch projections
As 0.05 but with updated 2024 fishery age composition data for N_Chile, SC_Chile, and Offshore_Trawl, and updated 2023 fishery length composition data for FarNorth
As 0.06 but with updated 2024 weight at age data for N_Chile, SC_Chile, and FarNorth fleets, and for their associated CPUE indices
As 0.07 but replaced SC_Chile_CPUE index (traditional absolute scaled CPUE by trip)
As 0.08 but replaced Peru_CPUE index
As 0.09 but updated AcousN 2024 index, with associated age composition and weight at age
Updated Model and Sensitivities
As 0.10 but with updated model (selectivity changes, recruitment) to 2024; 0.10 data file
As 1.00 but with flexibility in selectivity for Offshore fleet in 2021 (changed from 2022), a break in catchability in 2021 and reduced CV for the 2022 Offshore CPUE index
As 1.00 but with proposed standardised CPUE index from Vásquez & Sepúlveda (SC12-JM06), using CV = 0.2 and a 1% tech creep factor
As 1.02 but with stdev reported in the study (SC12-JM06; annually-varying CV ~ 0.1)
As 1.01 but with current year selectivity change implemented for N_Chile fleet
As 1.04 but with corrected AcousN survey data
As 1.05 but with a decreased CV (from 0.5 to 0.3) for AcousN survey
As 1.06 but with increased error for CPUE data in the current year (CV from 0.2 to 0.4)
